REQUEST TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818 (CONFIDENTIAL) FILED BY DEFENDANT MORAN, MICHAEL ORDER FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS GRANTED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68634 (E), CRC 3.52

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MORAN'S DEMURRER TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. PER COMMON PARLANCE AND UNDERSTANDING AS WELL AS THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, A LIEN IS NOT A CLAIM IN A LAWSUIT AND THUS THE FILING OF A LIEN BY THE CITY IN MR. MORAN'S LAWSUIT AGAINST YELLOW CAB DOES NOT CONSTITUTE "ANOTHER ACTION" FOR PURPOSES OF A DEMURRER BASED ON ANOTHER ACTION PENDING. MEDICAL LIEN LAW MAKES CLEAR THAT A MEDICAL PROVIDER'S ASSERTION OF A LIEN IN A PERSONAL INJURY LAWSUIT DOES NOT PRECLUDE THE MEDICAL PROVIDER FROM ASSERTING OTHER REMEDIES TO RECOVER FOR SERVICES IT PROVIDED. MR. MORAN'S RECOURSE, IF ANY, TO AVOID WHAT HE CONSIDERS DUPLICATIVE PROCEEDINGS IS TO SEEK A DISCRETIONARY STAY OF THIS ACTION UNTIL THE YELLOW CAB CASE IS RESOLVED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Debra D. Lew, Assistant Tax Collector Atty., Tel. No. 1-415-554-7888
appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Michael Moran's demurrer to both causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff City and County of San Francisco is overruled as to both causes of action. Per common parlance and understanding as well as the Code of Civil Procedure, a lien is not a claim in a lawsuit and thus the filing of a lien by the City in Mr. Moran's lawsuit against Yellow Cab does not constitute "another action" for purposes of a demurrer based on another action pending. Medical lien law makes clear that a medical provider's assertion of a lien in a personal injury lawsuit does not preclude the medical provider from asserting other remedies to recover for services it provided. Mr. Moran's recourse, if any, to avoid what he considers duplicative proceedings is to seek a discretionary stay of this action until the Yellow Cab case is resolved. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/hk
